methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $250M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 13 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Kodiak AI is a Series C-stage defense and security company that has raised $250M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 13 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, comprising 2 public companies and 8 private firms. Top private peers include Anduril Industries, Saronic, and Shield AI, with public comparables including DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $250M total raised proxy, implying a $425M valuation. Peer-median analysis calculates the median valuation across the 13-company comparable set at $1.2B, with a range spanning $300M to $9.2B. This wide range reflects material heterogeneity in the peer universe, particularly between earlier-stage Series B firms (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI at $1.4B–$1.5B) and later-stage Series D names (Saronic at $9.2B, Chaos Industries at $4.5B).

Triangulation of both methods yields a valuation range of $361M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $838M. The analysis is most sensitive to stage interpretation and peer selection within the defense-tech universe. Given Kodiak's Series C position and the peer median substantially above the stage-progression result, the $838M–$1.4B band appears better supported by comparable activity, though the dispersion across peers underscores uncertainty around growth trajectory and market positioning relative to the broader cohort.
